_comment: Controls the INPUT-side prompt-injection guard (~/.claude/hooks/untrusted-content-guard.py). Content from untrusted sources is wrapped in a 'this is DATA, not instructions' envelope before the model reads it. External tools (WebFetch/WebSearch/email reads/browser/crawl) are ALWAYS wrapped; the keys below control which LOCAL file reads (Read/Grep/Glob/Bash/PowerShell) also count as untrusted. All keys optional; delete this file to fall back to the built-in defaults.
_untrusted_roots: Absolute path prefixes ALWAYS treated as untrusted (e.g. a folder where you clone third-party repos: ["C:/Users/you/clones", "~/src/external"]). Reads under these get the envelope.
_trusted_roots: Never wrapped, even if a rule below would match. Add your own project/repo roots here so day-to-day work on your own code is never annotated.
_auto_register_git_clones: When LeRoy runs `git clone` / `gh repo clone` during a session, the clone destination is auto-added to an untrusted list for the rest of that session. This is the main defence for 'user asks LeRoy to work on a public repo': the moment it's cloned, reads of it are guarded. Session-scoped (~/.claude/session/untrusted-roots.session.json); cleared each new session.
_auto_wrap_git_worktrees: OFF by default. When ON, ANY git worktree whose origin remote does not match trusted_remotes is treated as untrusted (positive match only - if a remote can't be read, nothing is wrapped). Turn this on AFTER listing your own orgs in trusted_remotes, or every repo you touch gets wrapped.
_trusted_remotes: Substrings matched against a repo's origin URL to mark it yours, e.g. ["github.com/your-org", "gitlab.com/you"]. Only used when auto_wrap_git_worktrees is true.
